message:
feat(selection): add priority weighting and preferred worker set handling

Enhances worker selection algorithm with priority-based scoring and
improved handling of preferred workers for project-specific locality.

SelectionWeights changes:
- New priority weight (default 0.1) for explicit worker prioritization
- Enables configuration-based worker preference beyond just slots/speed

Preferred workers handling:
- Build HashSet of preferred worker IDs for O(1) lookups
- Separate eligible and preferred candidate lists during filtering
- If preferred workers are available and eligible, select from those
- Fall back to all eligible workers only when preferred are unavailable
- Debug logging when falling back from preferred to all eligible

Selection flow:
1. Filter by circuit state (skip Open, track HalfOpen)
2. Filter by slot availability (available > 0)
3. Filter by required runtime support
4. Partition into preferred and eligible lists
5. Select from preferred if non-empty, else from eligible
6. Apply circuit state penalty to final scores
7. Return highest scoring worker

This improves project-specific worker affinity by ensuring that if
a project's preferred workers are healthy, they will be selected
rather than potentially better-scoring but unfamiliar workers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
